page: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is a general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated CPU resources. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the instance is powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz. It provides 4 vCPUs, which map to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.91 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 160 GB local SSD for storage. No GPU accelerators are included, and the instance comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and bzip3 single-threaded decompression, both placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is weaker, with stress-ng multi-core, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is average, while read-only memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks is weak. Cryptographic capabilities are supported via hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI instruction sets.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation, the ccx23 offers predictable compute performance for workloads that do not heavily parallelize across many cores. It is suitable for text processing, basic single-threaded data decompression, and light web serving, though its database operations and static web serving throughput benchmark in the bottom 25%. From a resource density perspective, the instance provides dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio, offering a stable hardware profile for general-purpose workloads without the resource contention typical of shared-core budget instances.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is a general-purpose virtual server configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz. The instance features 16.0 GB of RAM, a 160 GB local SSD, and operates on the x86_64 architecture using the QEMU hypervisor. Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance and single-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core execution and memory read bandwidth fall into the bottom 25%. Lacking a GPU and complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this server is designed for workloads requiring dedicated, predictable single-threaded compute resources. It is best utilized for text processing, light application hosting, and general-purpose tasks that do not demand high multi-threaded throughput or intensive database operations.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is a dedicated AMD EPYC virtual server featuring 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 160 GB local SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is a dedicated AMD EPYC virtual server with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual server with balanced memory and strong single-core performance for predictable general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC processor with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
16.0 GB of system memory and 160 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Hardware-accelerated cryptography with AES and SHA_NI support
Applicable for text processing and predictable general-purpose workloads
